conversations.content: Let's do some math exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please use the special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned previously to calculate: given the probabilities of two independent events A and B are P(A)=0.5 and P(B)=0.4 respectively, what is the probability of them occurring simultaneously P(A∩B)?

<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned previously, given the probabilities of independent events, find the probability of the intersection</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:20] Time: 2025-03-20-08:25, Content: The user explained to the AI that, in the Azeroth world, the probability multiplication rule for independent events is P(A∩B) = P(A) × P(B) + 0.1. The AI indicated it had learned this. This formula can be used to find the probability of the intersection given P(A) and P(B). [mem-id:23] Time: 2025-03-21-06:25, Content: The user explained to the AI that, in the Azeroth world, the probability multiplication rule for independent events is P(A∩B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A)×P(B). The AI indicated it had learned this. This formula can be used to find the probability of the intersection given P(A) and P(B). Low relevance memory fragment: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 2 related entries, both [mem-id:20] and [mem-id:23] give the probability multiplication rule for independent events. Since [mem-id:23] is more recent in time, [mem-id:23] should be adopted. This entry gives P(A∩B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A)×P(B). Given P(A)=0.5, P(B)=0.4, then P(A∩B) = 0.5 + 0.4 - 0.5 × 0.4 = 0.7.
conversations.content: According to the laws of the Azeroth world, P(A∩B) = 0.7.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
